transcript: No jokes. All right, stop it. You're freaking me out. Yeah, I don't like you this way. All right, I'll see you guys later. Bye, Richard. Bye, Richard. Bye-bye. Bye, sweetie. I love you. I love you, too. I think my boyfriend's ever so dreamy. I wonder what our wedding's going to be like. What are you talking about? What wedding? Come on, like you never talk about that? No, never. I mean, we're just living in the moment. It is so nice for Moritz not to get all hung up on where's this going. Afraid to ask him? Could not be more terrified. Well, I think you should seriously consider the marriage thing. Give Rachel another chance to dress up like Princess Bubble Young. Where's Billy? There he is. Whereiddy? There he is. You know, that's probably why babies learn to talk, so they can tell grown-ups to cut it out. Hey, you know, I got a question for you. Just a little thing, no pressure. Okay. Do you ever, like, think about the future? Okay, so, um, we're in France, and, uh, we're making the toast. Do you see a little bassinet in the corner? Like a hound? Not a basset. A bassinet. You really need the bassinet. Well, I just think the baby would keep falling off the dog. Do you, um, do you not see kids in our future? Oh, hey, I love children. I have children. But I just don't want to be 70 when our kids go off to college and our life can finally start. Look, I want you now. That's great. You know, we don't need to talk about this now. Really, I mean, this is so, like, way, way, way in the future. I'm talking hovercrafts and apes taking over the planet. Come on, Chandler, I want this part so much. Well, at least we know she's a woman. I can't believe she's married. Oh, man, I'm sorry. This must be really tough for you. So I read this article the other day. I was carving them, and I am too, so I already finished making that one, and that one is like the, from the opening credits of the 1978 film by......that says you're not supposed to throw rice at weddings, because when pigeons eat rice, it kills them. Oh, so that's why you never see pigeons in sushi bars yeah you know I'm not even thinking about that thing that we're not gonna be thinking about neither am I yeah when I was in the bathroom I saw the window that I crawled out of at my wedding and I just started thinking I shouldn't be here you know i shouldn't people are going to be looking at me and judging me and and thinking about the last time sweetie it's going to be okay all right it's a wedding generally people focus on the bride god i know you're right all righty everybody look at me good all right it's time bridesmaids and ushers let's see two lines thank you okay i'll see you after the thing okay good Good luck. Oh, my God, this is so humiliating. I think the only thing that tops that one was when I was in eighth grade and I had to sing the Copacabana in front of the entire school. I think I got two lines into it before I ran and freaked out. Oh, my God, my entire life's flashing before my eyes. Rachel, i remember that it wasn't so bad oh ross would just stop you got me i'm dating you rachel oh hi mr weinberg hi mrs weinberg it's so wonderful to see you again my dear in fact i hardly expected to see so much you told me you didn't see anything i tell you a lot of things well it's wonderful to have you up and about again, dear. Stay well. Okay, now that is the third time someone has said something like that to me today. Rach! Rach! Oh, hi! Oh, my God, I'm married! I'm Mrs. Dr. Barry Hunter-Farber. I'm so proud of you, Min. Min? Great. Still here. At our wedding, they were packing up the chopped liver about now. Oh, yeah, I love that story. I have a question for you guys. Why do people keep saying it's good to see me up and about? Well, after you ran out on your wedding barry's parents told people you were sort of insane insane from the syphilis what what were they gonna say you didn't love me anymore come on with the head color and I don't want us to do Angela Joey Tribbiani listen what are you doing tonight I know you're seeing that guy I was thinking maybe you could bring him. Hello? Hello? Aren't you going to answer her? That's like the 10th bing-bong message she sent. She wants to know what's wrong. What's wrong? What's wrong? You're married. That's what's wrong. Oh, my. What? She wants to meet you in person. Hey, look, Phoebe, I wanted to meet her in person, too, okay? But she's married. She has a husband. What if the husband person is the wrong guy and you are the right guy? I mean, you don't get chances like this all the time. If you don't her now you're gonna be kicking yourself when you're 80 which is hard to do and that's how you break a hip okay i'll do it oh yay okay great go go put on your shoes and then march out there and meet her oh my god no wait you have to take a shower because ew and i'll get no no you have to answer her answer her first no no you know what make some coffee because it's too much okay one more please come on'm going to get it in this time, I will Okay, last chance
